modules.reading_paragraph: Force causes acceleration: The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it and inversely proportional to its mass. Key detail: The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it and inversely proportional to its mass. Key detail: Resultant force (F) and acceleration (a) are directly proportional; if you double the resultant force acting on an object, you double its acceleration. modules.fallback: Switch Resultant Force Calculation to audio plus two flip cards with one sentence each.
modules.reading_paragraph: Resultant Force Calculation: When two forces act in the same direction, the resultant force is the sum of the individual forces. Key detail: When two forces act in opposite directions, the resultant force is the difference between the magnitudes of the two forces, and its direction is the same as the larger force. Key detail: When two forces act in the same direction, the resultant force is larger than either individual force, and it is calculated by adding their sizes together. Key detail: A resultant force is the single force acting on the object when all the individual forces have been combined.
